reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: The demo runs a REAL in-browser object-detection pass: worker.js imports the shared loadPipeline (lib/webai.js) and calls a TWO-STAGE DETR pipeline: worker.js runs Xenova/table-transformer-detection (find tables) and structure-worker.js runs Xenova/table-transformer-structure-recognition (rows/columns/headers/cells) — both object-detection pipe(imageURL, {threshold, percentage:false}) in their own module Workers. No canned result. Grounded in the actual worker source + the immutable conformance suite, which tests `loads-clean` (console-clean, headless Chrome) and `calls-real-inference` (source pattern: pipeline(|createModelLoader|...). Stage one emits 'table' / 'table rotated' boxes; you click a detected table and stage two decomposes that crop into row/column/header/cell boxes at its own floor. Two real models (~30 MB each), not one. NOTE: this is a retrospective code+suite-grounded score — no fresh measured latency/screenshot was captured in this backfill pass, hence 4 not 5.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); Two separate module Workers (detection + structure); the page reconstructs the row×column grid from the returned part-boxes.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = (1) table boxes on the document, then (2) structure boxes (table row, table column, column header, projected row header, spanning cell) on the chosen table crop. Two canvases: <canvas id=stage> (document + clickable table boxes) and <canvas id=structStage> (crop + row/column/cell overlays). The grid is reconstructed for OCR-to-CSV.
rubric.notes: Grid reconstruction (rows × columns → cells) is a geometric heuristic over the structure model's part-boxes, not a model-native cell grid — an honest post-process of a real detection output.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/table-transformer-detection, linked to its HF card), the real pipeline task (object-detection), backend (WASM — no WebGPU needed), quantisation (q8), download size (~30 MB) and license (MIT). The worker's loadPipeline call and the createModelLoader model config both use the SAME id/dtype/sizeMB — no invented API surface.

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model:{modelId:'Xenova/table-transformer-detection', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:30, requiresWebGPU:false}) — no hand-rolled Load button. A valid current on-device model auto-initialises (checking → initialising → ready); only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance tests `shared-auto-init-loader` (source: createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/table-transformer-detection so the ~30 MB cache is tracked against the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/inference state is announced in role=status aria-live regions (2 aria-live=polite regions in the page). Failures surface on the page as a readable `.status.err` message ("Table detection failed: …"), never only in the console; the shared loader exposes Retry/re-download recovery. Runs on the WASM path everywhere — no WebGPU gate. Conformance tests `live-status-region`, `errors-on-the-page`.
rubric.notes: Two-stage flow: detect → select a table → recognise structure; each stage announces its own status.

rubric.evidence: Real keyboard-operable controls: file dropzone (role=button), sample document thumbnails (role=button, Enter/Space), a Detect button, a clickable/keyboard-focusable result canvas (tabindex=0, role=button — click a table to select it) that triggers structure recognition, and threshold sliders.

No canned/hardcoded output: every visible result is derived from the real worker run — both stages' boxes come from the two real object-detection passes; the grid is reconstructed from those boxes. Conformance carries `no-fake-output` (manual-evidenced) and `calls-real-inference`.

Stage-one canvas carries aria-label='Document with detected table boxes — click a table to select it, then recognise its structure' and is keyboard-focusable (tabindex=0, role=button); stage-two canvas carries aria-label='Cropped table with row, column and cell overlays'; sample thumbnails have alt text; buttons have names; status uses role=status aria-live. Conformance tests `images-have-alt` and `buttons-have-names` (both script assertions over the DOM).
rubric.notes: models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses the shared design system (public/styles.css) with fluid flex/grid, flex-wrap and intrinsic sizing; conformance carries `responsive-desktop` and `responsive-mobile` (no-horizontal-overflow at ≈1280×800 and ≈360×740 DPR3 touch) plus `parity-both-classes`.
rubric.notes: support.desktop/mobile are still needs-review in models.json (awaiting an agent parity verdict); models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); Both detection passes run in their own Workers; sliders filter cached boxes; grid reconstruction is a bounded reduction over the returned boxes. No main-thread inference or unbounded main-thread loop — INP stays low. Conformance carries `inference-off-main-thread` (source) and `inp-stays-low`.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive technology overlay boxes
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name and a text equivalent so assistive tech, translation and reading mode can reach it.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — BOTH canvases carry descriptive aria-labels; stage one is a keyboard-focusable role=button so the click-a-table interaction is operable without a mouse.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: two canvas aria-labels + tabindex=0/role=button on the document canvas

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the two detection models run in separate module Workers; sliders filter cached boxes; grid reconstruction is bounded.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live; every control keyboard-operable with a name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keep all controls keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live per stage, keyboard-operable canvas selection, named buttons, alt-texted thumbnails.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout no horizontal overflow fluid grid intrinsic sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — fluid flex/grid; both canvases inline-size:100% so neither overflows.

openQuestions: Grid reconstruction from part-boxes is a geometric heuristic — how robust is it to merged/spanning cells and rotated tables across the sample set?
Two models (~60 MB total) load; no fresh measured latency captured here. Sub-24px tap targets also need manual verification.

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a retained headless run of the two-stage flow (real table box + reconstructed grid + latency) so real-inference can be evidenced at 5.
Stress-test grid reconstruction on spanning/merged cells and rotated tables; improve the heuristic if it mis-groups.

summary: Retrospective code+conformance-grounded critique for table-transformer (Xenova/table-transformer-detection + -structure-recognition, object-detection, q8, ~30 MB each, MIT). A real TWO-STAGE DETR pipeline in two module Workers: detect tables → click one → recognise its row/column/header/cell structure, reconstruct the grid for OCR-to-CSV. Two labelled canvases (the document canvas keyboard-operable as role=button), shared auto-init loader, live status + on-page errors. Honest weaknesses: grid reconstruction is a geometric heuristic over part-boxes (io-shape note), ~60 MB of models, no fresh measured latency captured here, sub-24px tap-target flags at needs-review.
